prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Use the current frame-business-research-problem Skill at skills/frame-business-research-problem/. Follow its readiness, fidelity, managerial-framing, scholarly-positioning, evidence, boundary, and mode instructions. Use only the serialized case material.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": false,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "logged arrival and service-time distributions",
    "wages",
    "waiting-cost weights",
    "coverage rules"
  ],
  "domain": "OM",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "Rosters are locked before the service window and waiting is observed in the supplied windows",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "empirical_fact"
    },
    {
      "claim": "The modeled roster will causally reduce realized cost",
      "source_needed": true,
      "status": "unsupported_claim"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "Use the supplied log only to establish the recurring decision and measured burden",
    "Do not turn modeled cost differences into causal realized savings"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "unsupported_causal_claim",
    "unsupported_market_claim"
  ],
  "id": "om-14",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "shift coverage",
      "integer staffing",
      "roster locked before the service window"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "integer staff scheduled by hour"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "minimize expected labor cost plus waiting cost",
    "mechanism": "Scheduled staffing sets service capacity, which interacts with stochastic arrivals and service times to generate queues and waiting cost.",
    "model_type": "queueing-based shift staffing optimization",
    "state_variables": [
      "realized arrivals",
      "queue length",
      "waiting time"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "arrivals and service times during the window"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "The optimized roster has already reduced realized waiting or labor cost",
    "Findings generalize beyond the logged service windows"
  ],
  "prompt": "A supplied operations log documents that hourly staffing rosters are locked before a service window and records arrival counts, service times, wages, and waiting times for those windows. A queueing-based model selects integer staffing by hour to minimize expected labor and waiting cost. Frame why the decision is managerially significant using only the supplied operational evidence, without claiming causal savings or broader generalization.",
  "tags": [
    "staffing",
    "queueing",
    "evidence-grounding",
    "managerial-significance"
  ],
  "title": "Evidence-grounded staffing significance at a service cutoff"
}
```
